title: Staring at co-worker's chest is morally wrong but not voyeurism under IPC: Bombay High Court
description: Staring at a woman colleague’s chest in an office setting, though offensive, does not amount to the offence of voyeurism under Section 354C of the Indian Penal Code (IPC), the Bombay High Court recently held [Abhijit Baswant Nigudkar v. State of Maharashtra & Ors.].Thus, Justice Amit Borkar quashed a first information report (FIR) against a Max Life Insurance employee, one Abhijit Nigudkar, who was booked for voyeurism. “Unwanted staring, even if accepted as true, is not the same thing
